questions.text:
'Dusryachya manatle jaannaara' yaasaathi pudheelaiki yogya paryaay nivadaa.
a. Antardnyaanee
b. Antardhaan
k. Manakavadaa
d. Bhavishyavettaa
options: Fakat a barobar baaki sarv chook
Fakat a va k barobar baaki sarv chook
Fakat k barobar baaki sarv chook
Fakat d barobar baaki sarv chook

questions.text: Pudheelaiki konati sandhi yogya aahe?
Kaal kashaala mhantataat?
options: Kartaa, karma, kriyaapad yaanchya yogya vaaparalaa

questions.text: 'Udyaa aamhi suddha gaavaalaa jaanar aahet.' — Adhorekshit shabd pudheelaiki kontya prakaraateel aahe?
Pudheelaiki sanskrut upasargaghateet shabd kontaa?

Khaaleel visheshanaanche udaaharanashi yogya jodya laavaa.
a. Naam sadrushya visheshan - I. Jo mulagaa vyaayaam karto, to sashakt asto.
b. Darshak visheshan - II. Shreemant maanasaannaa garva asto.
k. Sambandhee visheshan - III. Kon manushya yeun gela.
d. Prashnarthak visheshan - IV. Hee mulagee chalaakh aahe.
'Sahaleemadhye shikshakaannee vidyaarthyaannaa sambhaalatate.' — Yaa vaakyaateel kartaa kontaa?
'Dev tyaache bhale karo' yaa vaakyaachaa prakaar kontaa?
'Tumchi shorlee mulagee yethe sukhroop pochali' yaa vaakyaateel uddeshy aani visheya olakhaa.

'Raamanujan yaannee avghad ganite atishay sahajane sodakoon daakhavalee.'
— Yaa vaakyaateel adhorekshit shabd he pudheelaiki konte ghatak aahet?

'Mee tyaalaa vichaarle aahe naa? Aamche aamhee paahoon gheu. Aamchyaamadhye _______ tulaa saangilatale konee?'
— Yaamadhye gaalavleleya jaagevar pudheelaiki kontaa vaakyaprachaar barobar therel?
'Dolyaavar kaatade odhane' yaa vaakyaprachaaraachya viruddha arth suchavnaara vaakyaprachaar pudheelaiki kontaa?
'Paalaadhyaa ghadyaavar paanee' yaa mhanitoon kaay suchavile jaate?
options: Yogya veli faayada millavata aala naahi.

questions.text:
Pudheelaiki 'mishra vaakya' konte?
a. Aamhee jaato amuchya gaavaa.
b. Aakaashaat jevhaa dag jamataat tevhaa mor naachoo laagato.
k. Saayankaalee mee kreedaanganaavar khelato kinvaa mitraambarobar phiraaylaa jaato.
d. Baabaa mhanaale kee New York he ek mothe shahar aahe.
options: a va k barobar aani b va d chook aahet
b va d barobar aani a va k chook aahet
a va d barobar aani b va k chook aahet
Fakat d barobar aani baaki sarv chook aahet

questions.text: 'Gautamne roj ek pustak vaachale.' — Yaa vaakyaache kartaree prayogaat roopantar kase hoeel?
options: a va b donheehi pramaane naahi

questions.text: (Passage-based) Maasikaaateel goshta kashaabaddalachi hotee?
(Passage-based) 'Bhugol' aani 'nakaashaa' yaa sankalpanaa shekado varshe vikasit hot hotya, kaaran —
options: Ekeka bhaugolik ghatakaachya maahiteesovat tappyaatappyaane aakalan vaadhat gele.
Sarvaannaa mahattva kaleparyant anek piddyaa gelya.
Prachand maahitee asalyaane golaa karne yaas hajaaro varshe laagalee.

questions.text: (Passage-based) Bhugolabaddalchee jaaneev va maanasaanchaa sanskrutik vikaas parasparsambandheet aahet, kaaran —
options: Bhaugolik dnyaanaachaa aadhaar bhupradeshaanvar varchassva milavatanaa milaalaa.
Bhaugolik dnyaanamule naisargik sampatteechaa aaplya sukhsoyeesaathee adhikadhik vaapar hou laagalaa.

questions.text: (Passage-based) Jaagatik raajakaaranavar bhugolachya dnyaanaachaa kaay parinaam zhalaa?
(Passage-based) Yaa utaaryaasaathee pudheelaiki konte sheershak yogya therel?
Pudheelaiki kontaa gat samaanaarthee shabdaanchaa aahe?

Pudheelaiki kontaa shabd arthadrushtyaa samaanaarthee shabdaataat basat naahee?
a. Mahilaa
b. Lalanaa
k. Savitaa
d. Naaree
options: Fakat a barobar baaki sarv chook
Fakat b barobar baaki sarv chook
Fakat a, b va d barobar baaki sarv chook
Fakat k barobar baaki sarv chook

questions.text:
(i) Gurujinee malaa prashna vichaarle.
(ii) Tine garebaannaa paise dile.
Vareel vaakyaateel kriyaapade kontyaa prakaarachee aahet?

Pudheelaiki kontyaa gataateel tatsam shabd aahet?
a. Pad, dugdh, graas
b. Ghar, haat, gaav
k. Karna, aranya, jal
Vaakyaateel kriyaapadachya roopavaroon keval kaalaachaa bodh hot asel tar tyaalaa _______ vaakya mhanataat.

"It is extremely hot today."
The underlined word is:
Identify the sentence written in correct passive voice.
options: Peter was taught to sing by Mr. Price.
Tim was started to respect by his colleagues.
I enjoyed taking the children to the zoo.
They saw him climbing over the fence.

questions.text:
"The Sun has ripened the fruit."
Which one of the following is the Passive Structure corresponding to the Active Voice structure above?
The verb form of the given noun word "Frost" will be:
Identify the correct conditional sentence.
options: If he left his bicycle, someone will steal it.
If you try again, you would succeed.
If he had written the letter, I'll post it.
If we had found him earlier, we could have saved his life.

questions.text:
"None of us knew the way, _______"
The correct tag-question for the sentence above is:

"The dog that barks does not bite."
The underlined words are:

Which one of the following is the one-word substitute of the given sentence.
"A decision which cannot be altered or withdrawn."

The word "Sanguine" is synonymous with the word _______.
a. Serious  b. Sadistic  c. Optimistic  d. Genuine

Adversities provoke one to _______ one's best efforts.
Select the correct alternative to complete the sentence meaningfully.

Choose the correct meaning of the idiom underlined in the following sentence.
She is leading a dog's life as her husband is an alcoholic and without a job.

They have a huge reserve of latent talent.
Identify the correct meaning of the underlined word.

Match the following pairs:
a. Eccentric - I. A person who believes in God
b. Infallible - II. A man of strange habits
c. Fanatic - III. A person who cannot make any mistakes
d. Theist - IV. A person with narrow religious views
Select the alternative with the words spelt correctly.

"I always see eye to eye with my sister."
The underlined idiom in the sentence above means:

(Passage on EV/Connected Mobility) The passage shows that
a. digital connectivity experts are concerned with vehicle users and consumers.
b. manufacturers of motors and motorcycles are thinking of increasing their customers.
c. the OEMs can easily adapt connected mobility.
d. it is easy for the government to promote electric mobility.

(Passage on EV/Connected Mobility) The integrated system of transportation should mainly think of
a. advancements in battery technology and power devices.
b. data transfer and data analysis.
c. modem for Connectivity and Bluetooth or Wi-Fi link.
d. Safety and experience of vehicle users.
(Passage on EV/Connected Mobility) Vehicle users are reluctant to use electric vehicles because
options: c only (concerned with cost of EVs and range)

questions.text:
(Passage on EV/Connected Mobility) One of the challenges discussed in the passage is about
a. how to integrate various features for connectivity.
b. how the vehicle user will access information about his geoposition.
c. how the government will promote electric mobility.
d. who will pay the maintenance cost of backend infrastructure.
(Passage on EV/Connected Mobility) The phrase 'by leaps and bounds' used in the passage means:

"He knows how to break in horses."
The underlined idiom in the sentence above means:

"The boy who is wearing a green shirt is my student."
In the given sentence, the underlined words describe:

"Shall I buy a scooter?"
The modal in the sentence above has been used to express:
Choose the correct sentence.
options: He has been working for two months.
He was slept when the thief broke in.
He has gone to Pune, yesterday.
We have gone there last week.

questions.text: Fill in the blank: "She has been missing _______ Monday last."
All districts of Maharashtra are divided into _______ administrative divisions.

Match the pair of States and Hydroelectric Project:
a. Karnataka - I. Durgapur
b. West Bengal - II. Gandhisagar
c. Odisha - III. Hirakud
d. Madhya Pradesh - IV. Tungabhadra
Which administrative division has the highest number of districts in Maharashtra?
Arrange the coal fields of India from North to South in the proper sequence.
options: Ranchi, Hazaribagh, Wardha Valley, Godavari Valley
Godavari Valley, Wardha Valley, Hazaribagh, Ranchi
Hazaribagh, Ranchi, Wardha Valley, Godavari Valley
Godavari Valley, Wardha Valley, Ranchi, Hazaribagh

questions.text: Tarapur nuclear power station is located in _______ taluka of Palghar district.

Examine the following statements and choose the correct option.
Statement A: The world's largest River Island 'Majuli' is in Sindhu river bed.
Statement B: In Tibet, river Brahmaputra is known as 'Dihang' and in Arunachal Pradesh, as 'Tsangpo'.
options: Statement A and Statement B both are correct.
Statement A is correct and Statement B is wrong.
Statement A is wrong and Statement B is correct.
Statement A and Statement B both are wrong.

questions.text:
Examine the following statements and choose the correct option.
Statement A: The hill range that spreads from Raireshwar to Shingnapur is known as Satpura hill range.
Statement B: Godavari and Tapi river basins are separated by Satmala – Ajanta hill range.
options: Statement A and Statement B both are correct.
Statement A is correct and Statement B is wrong.
Statement A is wrong and Statement B is correct.
Statement A and Statement B both are wrong.

questions.text:
Examine the following statements and choose the correct option.
Statement A: The First Sugar Factory was established at Rahuri in Ahmednagar district, in the year 1920.
Statement B: The First Co-operative sugar factory was founded at Loni (Pravaranagar) in Ahmednagar district in the year 1950.
options: Statement A and Statement B both are correct.
Statement A is correct and Statement B is wrong.
Statement A is wrong and Statement B is correct.
Statement A and Statement B both are wrong.

questions.text: Cluster settlements are found in _______ area of Maharashtra.

Match the pair of Column A (Peak) and Column B (Districts):
a. Astambha - I. Ahmednagar
b. Salher - II. Amravati
c. Chikhaldara - III. Nandurbar
d. Harishchandragad - IV. Nashik

Which essential conditions have been suggested by WTO for globalization?
a. No limit of geographical boundary for trade
b. Free capital movement
c. Free flow of technology
d. Free international movement of labour
The 1993-94 budget had reduced the maximum rate of import duty from 110 percent to _______ percent.

Which things are expected in the privatization process?
a. To open public sector for private investment
b. Free trade policy
c. Special Economic Zone
d. Government industry ownership transfer to private sector
India's Foreign Exchange Reserve was _______ in June 1991.
_______ deals in procurement, distribution, export and import of selected agricultural commodities.
Under the Liberalisation, Privatisation, Globalisation (LPG) Policy, Government of India allowed 100 percent FDI in _______ sector.
The Headquarter of World Trade Organization is at _______.
Due to fall in dollar value of exports by 1.5 percent in 1991-92, the exports grew by over _______ percent in 1993-94.

Privatisation is related to _______.
a. Quality
b. Non-corruption
c. Efficiency
d. Disinvestment
The Government hands over the PSU's to private sector, which means _______.
Which of the following statements is incorrect about Legislative Council?
options: 1/3rd of its members are elected by local bodies.
It is not subject to dissolution.
President has the final power to decide about its composition.
Minimum strength of Legislative Council cannot be less than 40.

questions.text:
Consider the following statements about Gram Panchayats in Maharashtra:
A. A motion of no-confidence against Sarpanch may be moved by not less than one-third of the total number of the members of Gram Panchayat.
B. No such motion of no-confidence shall be brought within a period of three months from the date of election of Sarpanch.
options: Statement A is correct B is false.
Statement B is correct A is false.

questions.text: Which one of the following is not an 'All-India Service'?
Who has the right to remove the Judges of High Court from their post?
options: c only (President through the process of impeachment)

questions.text: Which of the following Amendment Act of the Indian Constitution added provisions mainly regarding Panchayats?

Match the following pairs:
a. Baba Padamji - I. Starting of the Newspaper 'Sudharak'
b. Savitribai Phule - II. Starting the weekly 'Prabhakar'
c. Gopal Ganesh Agarkar - III. The collection of poetry 'Bavan Kashi Subodh Ratnakar'
d. Bhau Mahajan - IV. The novel 'Yamuna Paryatan'
Mahatma Jyotiba Phule has praised the three basics of Liberty, Equality and Universal Brotherhood in his book _______.

Anutai Wagh worked for the upliftment of Tribal Society.
a. Establishment of Tribal Seva Mandal
b. Installation of Koswad Project
c. Beginning of Play School (Daycare Centre) and Montessory
d. Beginning of Educational Institutions
Which of the statements given above are correct?
Which of the following principles was not the principle of Arya Samaj?
options: Vedas are full of knowledge; spread the knowledge given in Vedas.

questions.text: Who wrote the book 'Vital Vidhwansak'?
Which among the following countries, has decided to use India as a base to address their food security concerns?

India shares maritime border with _______ countries.
Marathi feature film 'Panghrun' is based on a short story by _______.
Amar Jawan Jyoti was built as a tribute to Indian soldiers after the _______.

Which of the following countries was not part of the European Union as of March 2022?
Who wrote the book 'A Little Book of India: Celebrating 75 years of Independence'?
Who formed the Abhinav Bharat, a revolutionary organization?
_______ was not a follower of Jyotirao Phule or his Satyashodhak Samaj.

Match the pair event and related year:
a. Partition of Bengal - I. 1909
b. Establishment of Muslim League - II. 1916
c. Separate Electorates Act - III. 1905
d. Lucknow Pact - IV. 1906
The Father of Indian Workers Movement is _______.

Objectives of the Indian National Congress:
a. To make the people of India forget the differences in religion, race, caste, language, geographical territories and bring them on a common platform.
b. To deliberate the problems of Indians.
c. To increase the feelings of oneness among the Indians.
d. To put in efforts for Indian Independence.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
Kakori incidence was related with _______.
Who was the first President of Indian National Congress?

He organised the Ramoshi's and revolted against the British.
a. Vasudev Balwant Phadke  b. Ram Singh Kuka
c. Birsa Munda  d. Kajar Singh
